subject: Physics
title: Nikola Tesla: The Forgotten Genius
description: A quick, practical guide to Nikola Tesla: The Forgotten Genius.

hook.text: Nikola Tesla invented AC power, the radio, and envisioned wireless energy—then died alone and nearly penniless.

buildup.text: Tesla emigrated from Serbia to the US in 1884 with four cents and a letter of introduction to Edison.

discovery.text: He developed the AC induction motor and polyphase power system—the foundation of modern electrical grids.

twist.text: Edison tried to discredit Tesla's AC system by publicly electrocuting animals. Tesla's superior technology still won.

climax.text: Tesla filed over 300 patents but was terrible at business. He died in 1943 in a New York hotel room.

punchline.text: 300 patents. Invented the modern grid. Died forgotten. History caught up.

quiz.question: What was Tesla's most impactful contribution to electrical engineering?
options: The AC induction motor and polyphase power system
